pages.full_text: aspargus & strawberries. lovely large old oaks; beeches + [struck-through word] sweet chestnut trees around the lake.
photo_captions: Bucking Hall, Norfolk, The Long Gallery
pages.notes: Top of page is a continuation of text from the previous spread. A large colour photograph occupies most of the page, showing a long gallery interior. Caption written vertically alongside photo reads 'Bucking Hall, Norfolk, The Long Gallery'. One word in the top text is struck through (between 'beeches +' and 'sweet') — likely 'limes' or similar; could not read it clearly.

pages.full_text:
We drove south to near Halesworth. The campsite owner is friendly & has sold me some eggs. Mindon House* * * * Blyford. loo in farm house laundry room, no shower, lovely big field, walk in [struck-through word] meadow, big fish to be fished in pond at end of walk.
     Sun again in morning. We leave at 8.30. to pick up lampshades from Halesworth. Toni sat outside the van & watched while we loaded lampshades. Then we set off for Oundle on the R. Nene to Fairline Marina where Chad is hoping to do lights. We had a good pub lunch - Simon home-made beefburgers & Eve scampi & chips at Upper Benefield in a shady garden. On to Corby where Chad hopes a factory will do extruded aluminium. At tea time we passed the Buddhist Centre at near Naseby. The van is dirty, covered in squashed flies.
     There was a very smart woman dressed in Buddhist colours (& matching handbag) having tea. On the way into the house from the garden she blew on her arms twice to clear the
pages.notes: One word in the text is struck through (before 'meadow'); likely 'mud' or 'wet'. The campsite is called 'Mindon House' at Blyford — could also read as 'Menden House' or 'Minden House'; 'Mindon' is best reading. 'Fairline Marina' on the R. Nene near Oundle — could be 'Fairline' the boat manufacturer. Text cuts off at bottom of page (continues on next spread). No year directly on this page; context from volume B and surrounding pages suggests this is likely mid-2000s but cannot be confirmed from page content alone.
